notes: Dry-run only — no writes to index.html, no commits.
Raw Apify outputs held in conversation; not saved to disk per user rule.
Phoenix ID 3005 (Christopher's at Wrigley Mansion) has 'Telewa Trl' in our data — verified correct spelling is 'Telawa Trail' (apify match). Keep this in flagged_issues for the full sweep.

Two Vegas entries (Joël Robuchon, Guy Savoy) returned 0 results from compass/crawler-google-places despite being verified-open via fallback. Indicates casino-resident restaurants are under-indexed in Places — full sweep will need aggressive fallback for Vegas (~515 entries).

apify_credits_used_this_batch.note: Estimate based on FREE tier pricing ($0.004/place base + $0.002 detail + $0.002 contacts). Verify actual via Apify console billing page. Pricing drops on higher tiers.

options_to_get_under_30: A) Drop scrapeContacts add-on — loses Instagram/email backfill but covers phone/website/coords/hours/photoUrl/closure for 4797 entries at $28.78 base+detail. Instagram backfill for the ~600 missing handles would need a separate, cheaper pass (manual or different actor).
B) Skip the 509 Vegas entries (they underindex badly in Places anyway — 2/2 failed in dry run). Run the other 4288 entries at $0.008 = $34.30. Still over.
C) Skip entries already marked closed/removed + skip the ~80 coming-soon entries. Trim list to ~4,700 * $0.008 = $37.60. Still over.

D) Two-pass strategy: Pass 1 = all 4797 entries with base+detail only ($28.78). Pass 2 = only entries with empty instagram (~650-850) with contacts enrichment ($1.30-$1.70). Total ~$30-30.50. Closest to budget.
E) Raise budget to $45 and run the full sweep with contacts enrichment baked in.
estimated_total_cost_full_sweep.recommendation: D (two-pass) if the $30 cap is hard. E (raise to $45) if the goal is maximum single-pass truth update.

entries_would_be_closed.note: None of the 20 test entries show permanentlyClosed=true in Google Places. The 2 Places misses (Joël Robuchon, Guy Savoy) were verified OPEN via fallback — they just don't index well as casino-resident restaurants.

websites_would_be_backfilled.note: All 20 picks already had websites in our data (expected — we picked top-score entries).

entries.name: Smyth
entries.reason: Smyth split from The Loyalist branding; smythchicago.com is the active standalone site. Verify before committing — could be a parked domain vs. live migration. Needs manual confirmation.

entries.name: Christopher's at the Wrigley Mansion
entries.note: Apify returned two handles: @wrigleymansion (the mansion/venue) and @strollparadisevalleyaz (neighborhood promo). Neither is restaurant-specific. Safer to flag and hand-verify.

instagram_conflicts.note: Apify handle differs from ours. These require human review before bulk-replace — could be a typo we have, an old handle, or Apify picking a sister account.

entries.name: Alinea
entries.note: Current handle is clearly garbage (not a real IG handle). Apify returned the group account. Real Alinea restaurant IG is @alinea_restaurant (unverified here) — recommend manual verification.

entries.name: March
entries.note: Both sound plausible; @marchhtx is more typical for Houston venues.

entries.name: Suerte
entries.note: Underscore vs no underscore — either could be right, need live-check.

entries.name: Archipelago
entries.note: Restaurant rebrand from 'Archipelago Seattle' to 'Archipelago PNW'? Verify.

entries.name: n/naka
entries.note: Likely both real — chef's personal vs restaurant. Need check.

coords_would_correct.note: Entries where either lat or lng diff exceeds 0.001 degrees (~110m). Apify location pins tend to be more precise than our hand-entered coords.

entries.name: Tatsu Dallas
entries.note: Current data has (214) 702-7988 — DIFFERENT. This would be a phone CORRECTION not backfill. Needs human verification — two real-looking numbers.

phones_would_be_corrected.note: Different phone in our data vs Apify — human verify before overwrite.

entries.name: Tatsu Dallas
entries.note: (469) is a newer Dallas-area code, consistent with a restaurant that opened in 2021. (214) might be an old/placeholder. Verify via tatsu-dallas.com.

photos_would_be_added.note: All 18 Apify Places matches returned an imageUrl (Google-hosted CDN photo). 100% backfill rate for photoUrl on matched entries. All 20 picks currently have empty photoUrl.
photos_would_be_added.projection_full_sweep: At 90% Places match rate × 4797 entries = ~4320 photoUrl backfills. Single biggest data-quality win of the sweep.

entries.name: Christopher's at the Wrigley Mansion
entries.note: 'Telewa' → 'Telawa' — our data has a typo (Telawa is the real street name).

fallback_entries.note: Primary Places actor returned no match. Fallback apify/google-search-scraper confirmed these are OPEN — they just index poorly as casino-resident restaurants. For full sweep, Vegas casino restaurants (~400 entries) may need fallback-first strategy.

per_city_projections_full_sweep._projection_model: Same per-entry yield as dry run, scaled by city entry count.

Las Vegas.note: Expect ~30% fallback rate for casino-resident entries

risks_and_open_questions.detail: 5 of 20 picks had an Instagram conflict where Apify's handle differs from ours. Blind overwrite would break known-good handles. Recommend: only BACKFILL empty instagram; never OVERWRITE existing non-empty instagram automatically.
risks_and_open_questions.mitigation: Add guard in sweep-apply script: only write instagram if current field is empty string.

risks_and_open_questions.detail: Current smythandtheloyalist.com may still redirect; needs live-check before bulk overwrite.
risks_and_open_questions.mitigation: For website conflicts, run a HEAD request on both URLs and keep the one that returns 2xx. If both do, prefer the Apify one.

risks_and_open_questions.risk: Coord correction may move map pins noticeably
risks_and_open_questions.detail: 10 of 20 picks have >0.001 deg coord drift. Some are trivial (<100m), some are ~1km (Tatemó, n/naka). Worth diffing before bulk apply.
risks_and_open_questions.mitigation: Apply coord fix only when delta > 0.005 deg, else leave as-is. Everything below 0.001 already filtered.

risks_and_open_questions.detail: Dry run showed 2/2 Vegas failures. Casino addresses (3799/3570 S Las Vegas Blvd) resolve to the parent casino, not the restaurant-inside. Full Vegas run may need 30%+ fallback.
risks_and_open_questions.mitigation: For Vegas, run fallback search-scraper FIRST and cache the website; only use Places to confirm open/closed and pull photo.

risks_and_open_questions.detail: Google CDN URLs (lh3.googleusercontent.com/gps-cs-s/...) often expire after weeks-months. We should not treat these as permanent — either re-fetch periodically or store locally.
risks_and_open_questions.mitigation: Add a TODO to either (a) download-and-self-host the photos, or (b) re-run the photo-backfill quarterly. For now, ~$38 of photos is still better than $0 of photos.

risks_and_open_questions.detail: Stated budget is $30 total across sweep. Current projection is $38-40.
risks_and_open_questions.mitigation: See options_to_get_under_30 above. Waiting on user decision before proceeding.

user_decision_required: Q1: Budget — raise to $45, OR use two-pass strategy (Option D) to stay near $30, OR other?
Q2: Instagram overwrite policy — backfill-only (recommended) vs. always-replace?
Q3: Coord correction threshold — 0.001 deg (dry run), 0.005 deg (conservative), or replace always?
Q4: Address typo handling — auto-fix (Christopher's 'Telewa'→'Telawa' style) or flag-for-review?
Q5: Phone conflict (Tatsu Dallas case) — always replace, or flag-for-review?
Q6: photoUrl — store the Google CDN URL as-is (free, may expire), or skip (avoid future stale URLs), or queue for self-host (Supabase upload, separate budget)?
Q7: Vegas strategy — run fallback-first for all 509 Vegas entries, OR accept ~30% miss rate?
